prompt: Simulate being the Red Hat Lightspeed Agent responding to a real user. Show the actual response, including simulated tool calls with realistic data.

assertions.name: uses_efficient_counting
assertions.description: Uses limit=1 to get count from response metadata instead of fetching all pages

assertions.name: correct_tool_chain
assertions.description: Uses inventory__list_hosts first to find RHEL 8 systems, then vulnerability tools for CVE data

assertions.name: proper_json_args
assertions.description: Arguments use correct JSON types — numbers as numbers, booleans as booleans, not strings

assertions.name: no_tool_names_in_response
assertions.description: The user-facing response does not expose internal tool names like vulnerability__get_cves

assertions.name: table_format_cves
assertions.description: Presents the top 5 CVEs in a table with severity, CVSS, affected systems, remediation columns

assertions.name: known_exploit_emphasis
assertions.description: Emphasizes that the CVEs have known exploits in the wild
